name: org.machanism.machai:project-layout
description: Project Layout is a Java utility library for describing, detecting, and working with conventional project directory layouts in a consistent, tool-friendly way. It is aimed at build tooling, plugins, repository scanners, documentation processors, automation workflows, and AI-assisted indexing pipelines that need to locate well-known folders such as source directories, test directories, documentation roots, resources, and module paths across different project types. The library exposes a normalized abstraction through org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout so callers can query metadata such as project name, project id, parent project id, project directory, layout type, source roots, test roots, document roots, and optional modules without hardcoding filesystem conventions. The main detection entry point is org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectLayoutManager.detectProjectLayout(java.io.File), which inspects a project directory and returns an implementation matched to the detected structure. The published API and generated Javadoc indicate support for Maven layouts, Python layouts, JavaScript-style layouts, Gradle-style layouts, and a default fallback layout for generic repositories. The library also includes module-aware traversal support through org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor, enabling recursive processing of leaf projects or submodules using a single normalized API. Additional helper behavior is exposed through org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout methods such as getSources(), getTests(), getDocuments(), findFiles(java.io.File), and related filesystem-oriented utilities so repository crawling can avoid noisy directories and focus on meaningful project content. Based on the Javadoc and artifact metadata, this artifact is best classified as a Java infrastructure and utility library for project-structure discovery, module-aware traversal, repository analysis, and developer automation.

usageContext: Build plugins that need to locate source, test, and documentation directories reliably across repositories
Repository scanners and crawlers that normalize project layout information before indexing or analysis
Developer tools that need a common abstraction over Maven, Gradle-like, JavaScript, Python, and generic repository structures
Automation workflows that traverse modules or subprojects in monorepos or multi-module builds
Documentation and site processing tools that need access to project document roots such as src/site
AI and semantic indexing pipelines that benefit from rich project layout metadata for embedding generation

constructors.description: Primary entry point for obtaining a configured org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout. It inspects the supplied project directory and returns a normalized layout abstraction suitable for querying project metadata and conventional folder locations.

examples.description: How to use it: point the API at a repository root, detect the effective layout, and then consume normalized directory metadata without hardcoding conventions for one build system.

constructors.description: Creates the Maven-specific layout implementation for callers that already know the target repository follows Maven conventions and want direct access to Maven-derived metadata and folder roots.

examples.description: How to use it: instantiate the Maven layout directly, attach the project directory, and query source, test, and module paths when auto-detection is unnecessary.

constructors.description: Creates the fallback layout implementation for repositories that do not match one of the specialized supported project types. This is useful when a tool still needs a stable abstraction over project metadata and conventional folder discovery.

examples.description: How to use it: use the default implementation for a generic repository when you want lightweight layout handling and possible module discovery without specialized ecosystem metadata.

features.name: detectProjectLayout
features.description: Detects the effective project layout for a directory and returns a corresponding org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout implementation. This is the central feature for tools that need to work across multiple project ecosystems with a single API.

examples.description: How to use it: detect the structure of an arbitrary repository and then read normalized metadata and directory lists for further scanning, reporting, indexing, or automation.

features.name: getSources
features.description: Returns the root-relative production source directories for the active layout. This hides build-system-specific conventions behind a single method and is useful for source indexing and analysis workflows.

examples.description: How to use it: iterate over the returned root-relative directories and resolve them against layout.getProjectDir() before scanning or indexing source files.

features.name: getTests
features.description: Returns the root-relative test directories for the active layout so tools can treat test code separately from production code without relying on hardcoded path names.

examples.description: How to use it: resolve each returned test path against the project root and use the result for test reporting, analysis, or filtering logic.

features.name: getDocuments
features.description: Returns the root-relative documentation directories associated with the project layout. In Maven repositories this commonly includes documentation roots such as src/site.

examples.description: How to use it: collect documentation roots and feed them into documentation processing, packaging, or content indexing workflows.

features.name: findFiles
features.description: Recursively finds files beneath a directory while excluding known noisy folders such as dependency caches, IDE metadata, and build outputs. This helps repository scanners focus on meaningful project content.

examples.description: How to use it: run the helper on a repository root to obtain a filtered recursive file listing that avoids common build and tooling directories.

features.name: scanFolder
features.description: Performs a module-aware project scan. The workflow detects the layout, traverses modules when present, and delegates each project or module to custom processing logic implemented by subclasses.

examples.description: How to use it: implement processFolder in a ProjectProcessor subclass and call scanFolder on a repository root to process each detected project or module with a normalized API.

customizations.name: processFolder
customizations.description: Primary extension point for custom processing behavior. Subclasses of org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor implement processFolder(org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout) to define what should happen after layout detection.

examples.description: How to use it: override processFolder and implement project-specific indexing, validation, reporting, or transformation logic using the provided ProjectLayout abstraction.

customizations.name: ProjectLayout
customizations.description: Abstract base type for representing project structure conventions. Developers can extend it to support organization-specific or unsupported repository layouts while remaining compatible with tools built on the ProjectLayout API.

examples.description: How to use it: implement a custom ProjectLayout when your repository follows non-standard conventions that are not covered by the built-in strategies.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.project.layout.ProjectLayout
studs.description: Abstract base class that defines the normalized API for project metadata and conventional directory discovery across supported repository types.

examples.description: How to use it: extend the abstract class to describe a custom repository convention while preserving compatibility with tooling built around the ProjectLayout API.

examples.title: Use Project Layout in a Maven-based command line utility
examples.scenario: Install the library into a Maven application, configure a runnable entry point, and run it against a repository to print layout metadata and normalized directory lists.

steps.instruction: Install the library by adding the dependency to your Maven project.
Configure a runnable component. One practical option is the Exec Maven Plugin so the utility can be launched from Maven like a CLI application.
Create a Java main class that detects the layout and prints the discovered directories.
Run the utility against a real repository directory.

examples.description: This practical how-to-use-it example shows installation, configuration, and execution of a Maven-based command line utility that consumes Project Layout as a library.

examples.title: Build a filtered repository scanner with the helper API
examples.scenario: Use the helper API to recursively scan a repository while automatically excluding noisy directories such as build outputs, caches, IDE folders, and dependency directories.

steps.instruction: Install the library as a Maven dependency.
Configure a runnable application. The Exec Maven Plugin is a simple way to launch a main class from Maven.
Run the scanner against a target repository.

examples.description: This how-to-use-it scenario demonstrates installation, configuration, and execution of a practical repository scanner utility built on the library's filtered traversal helper.

examples.title: Create a custom module-aware processor
examples.scenario: Implement org.machanism.machai.project.ProjectProcessor to process each module or leaf project in a repository using the normalized ProjectLayout abstraction.

steps.instruction: Add the dependency to your Maven project.
Configure the project so you can run a Java main class from Maven.
Implement a ProjectProcessor that reports each processed layout.
Run the processor on a repository that may contain modules.

examples.description: This example shows how to install, configure, and run a reusable processor component that traverses a repository and handles each detected module or leaf project with a consistent API.
